review:
Doom Eternal is, without a doubt, my favorite first-person shooter of all time when it comes to gameplay. It's brutal, intense, gory and ridiculously fast-paced. You really feel like an unstoppable killing machine. Doom Guy is so simple minded and literally too angry to die, and the game does an incredible job of making you feel that rage and adrenaline as you tear through hordes of demons.

The gameplay is insanely well executed. You have dashes, double jumps, the super shotguns meat-hook, which lets you grapple through the air or launch yourself directly into demons, for a more close and personal destruction. There are lots of unique weapons and mods in your arsenal, and I do love how I actually find a use for all of them (excluding the Unmaykr). Everything flows perfect together. Need ammo? Chainsaw a fodder demon. Need health? Perform glory kills. speaking of glory kills, I absolutely love how much effort they put into them. With nearly 300 brutal animations, they perfectly showcase just how powerful and furious the Doom Slayer is, although I do wish the berserk power-ups had more unique ones, as it feels very limited in comparison.

Another huge part of what makes this game so incredible is the soundtrack, composed by Mick Gordon. The gritty, aggressive metal music fits the game perfectly and amplifies the intensity of every fight. The soundtrack constantly keeps your adrenaline pumping and makes every battle more chaotic and badass.

As for the story, while that's not why you play a Doom game, Eternal and The Ancient Gods DLCs do expand the lore quite a bit. The main plot revolves around Doom Slayer trying to eliminate hell's influence on earth by hunting down the three Hell Priests and stopping the demonic invasion. There's also the Khan Maykr, who made a deal with Hell to sacrifice worlds, including the life on earth, to create Argent Energy in order to sustain, and power her home world, Urdak. Doom Slayer takes it upon himself to stop all of it with his own hands. The story works well for a game primarily focused on action.

Even though it is my favorite FPS, the game isn't perfect. While the gameplay is, some of the bosses feels a bit underwhelming or poorly executed, especially the final boss who is basically just a bullet sponge. There are also occasional bugs, like the soundtrack not trigger properly or enemies failing to spawn, forcing you to restart from a checkpoint.

Still, Doom Eternal is just unbelievably fun to play. I've honestly lost count on how many times I've beaten it, yet I always find myself coming back for another playthrough just to get that adrenaline rush again. I highly recommend checking out all three of the modern Doom games, they are all worth playing, since each one feels unique while still being incredibly good in it's own way.

review:
Heavy metal, sangre, demonios, no necesito más. Cuánto he disfrutado este juego. El gameplay es maravilloso, los niveles están super bien diseñados y no se sienten para nada repetitivos. El diseño de los demonios es muy original. Cada uno tiene una mecánica única tanto para atacarte como sus puntos débiles.
La jugabilidad es maravillosa y muy frenética. La velocidad del juego está muy bien adaptada. La forma de conseguir munición es abrir a un demonio a la mitad con una motosierra ¿Qué hay mejor que eso? Las batallas tienen la suficiente cantidad de enemigos para disfrutar de un buen rato de exterminio. Además, de vez en cuando hay zonas más tranquilas que ayudan a mi cerebro a no explotar por la dopamina.
Sin duda alguna de mis juegos favoritos, muy muy recomendable. No se si jugaré a otro juego de la franquicia, no me disgustaría.

La forma de conseguir munición es abrir a un demonio a la mitad con una motosierra Qué hay mejor que eso? Las batallas tienen la suficiente cantidad de enemigos para disfrutar de un buen rato de exterminio. Además, de vez en cuando hay zonas más tranquilas que ayudan a mi cerebro a no explotar por la dopamina. Sin duda alguna de mis juegos favoritos, muy muy recomendable. No se si jugaré a otro juego de la franquicia, no me disgustaría.
review_english: Heavy metal, blood, demons, I don't need more. How much I enjoyed this game. The gameplay is wonderful, the levels are super well designed and they don't feel repetitive at all. The design of the demons is very original. Each one has a unique mechanic both to attack you and their weak points. The gameplay is wonderful and very frenetic. The speed of the game is very well adapted. The way to get ammo is to cut a demon in half with a chainsaw. What's better than that? The battles have enough enemies to enjoy a good time of extermination. Plus, from time to time there are calmer areas that help my brain not explode with dopamine. Without a doubt one of my favorite games, highly recommended. I don't know if I'll play another game in the franchise, I wouldn't be upset.

review: DOOM Eternal cranks everything from the 2016 reboot up to absurd levels and somehow makes it work. The combat is faster, more aggressive, and demands you constantly juggle weapons, glory kills, chainsaw refills, and flame belch in this insane rhythm that becomes genuinely addictive once it clicks. Some people bounced off the platforming, and yeah, it's a divisive choice, but personally I loved how it kept the pacing relentless. The soundtrack is god-tier, the world expands the lore in wild directions, and ripping and tearing has never felt better. id Software at the top of their game.

review:
It's a well-crafted and very solid FPS experience, polished across the board, great graphics, design, sound, etc etc. Good tactile feel with visceral, powerful and purposeful animations. Also incredibly well-optimized (wonderful performance on my RTX 3070) on top of being a very pretty game with some very pretty skyboxes.

Criticism (in my view) would be how curated the game's overall design is, that is, the amount of friction you'll experience relative to player agency, it's quite high (the meme about the game 'forcing' you to 'play a certain way', this is a basic complaint, but not wholly wrong, it's just most people can't articulate the thought properly.)

Basically, it's a very prescriptive game that has a high skill-floor, which personally I don't like all that much, and it's notably limiting on player expression without invoking a higher-than-average amount of punishment from its encounters (compared to most other games.)

I'd still like to get better at it because I really enjoy multiplicity in games, I just lean more towards stuff like DMC, Monster Hunter, proper boomer shooters, due to their comparatively higher 'freedom' to engage with encounters, but again, that's wholly preferential. It's good to try and outgrow your comfort zone, and learn to appreciate other genres/types of design.

review:
This game literally ruined all other First Person Shooters for me.  Anytime I pick up a new FPS I always end up comparing it to this one, and they all come up short (Including the latest addition to the Doom franchise).

This game starts off slow.  First, you're handed a pump shotgun with a few mods.  Then, you're given the heavy cannon with a precision mod that can destroy demon weakpoints.  Then, you're handed a grenade launcher that can stagger demons.  All of this happens in the first level.  Then, throughout the game, you're given more tools, more ways to express your skill and kick some demon ass in scenarios that would have seemed impossible to you a few hours prior.  The game flawlessly leads you down the path of mastery, and, by the end of the game, you're able to truly earn the title of DOOM SLAYER.

Also, this game runs at 400 FPS with Raytracing on.  Really puts the FUN in FUNctional.

review:
There's a routine I engage in when I'm about to write a review. I play the game. Set it down for a few months (or in this case a few years) and then review it. The idea is to dodge the dopamine hit — the flashy core mechanic, the bait-and-switch graphics — and arrive at a verdict that has aged like a bottle of Domaine Leroy (1949). In the interim, I get to read ♥♥♥♥ takes in the reviews. Speaking of that...

If a reviewer (paid critic or otherwise) marks down a game for a specific enemy and can't tell you which mechanic makes that enemy have a bad design, they're not reviewing the enemy. They're ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ a couch and then complaining that it doesn't feel like the real thing. It's a self-own: they're writing about themselves and calling it a review. Their citation to the effects of the mob on [i]their[/i] gameplay, instead of the effects of the mob's mechanics on gameplay [i]in general[/i], is the dead giveaway that the opinion has no bearing on [b]you[/b].

This situation applies singularly to the Marauder in Doom Eternal. Critics on Steam and on Medium[dot]com (presented in order of competency) have, since release, been going full pygmy marmoset, flinging their excrement of inadequacy at this miniboss:

[i]He breaks the run-and-gun.[/i]
[i]His shield prevents me from W-keying the game.[/i]
[i]I have to adjust my combat to take him into account.[/i]

The thing to recognize here is that these aren't critiques of an enemy, they're a confession by projection of the player, and the confession is that they shouldn't be publishing reviews anywhere. One should welcome an enemy who forces an adjustment, because that adjustment is the entire mechanism by which a combat game avoids feeling stale and programmatic. The Marauder is the moment Doom Eternal stops being a power fantasy (which, and I'll fight you on this, the series never was) and starts being a challenge. Marking it down for that moment is marking down the genre for being the genre.

The irony is that the people marking the game down for the Marauder are missing that the Marauder [b][i]is[/i][/b] the game: he's a microcosm of the entire design philosophy.

The Marauder specifically, and Doom Eternal generally, demands you be on your A+ game 100% of the time. It is a fast game. It is a twitch game. It is a game that requires you to predict N=2 steps in advance. The keyboard is a Bösendorfer Imperial 290 and the game demands you play  Beethoven's Klaviersonate Nr. 14 in cis-Moll, op. 27, Nr. 2 "Sonata quasi una fantasia", Beethoven-Werkverzeichnis-equivalent, third movement marked Presto agitato. It is, therefore, a game that rewards hypersituational awareness, fast reflexes, and a questionable dependency on second-hand-market Adderall. 

This is what elevates the game from a boomer shooter to Doom. It's the trust that id has for the gamers that it's been entertaining since Carmack decided that Commander Keen grew up, joined the marines, and got deployed to Mars to fix the UAC's mistake.

But, having said that, Doom Eternal is not a game without complaints.

I spent the first half of my playthrough convinced that id and Bethesda had lost their minds. The heavy emphasis on arenas. The resource-loop economy that herds you into a predetermined battle method if you don't want to scrounge for ammo mid-fight. The story drifting further and further from the series' founding mythos. None of this is Doom 2016. Doom 2016 was a shooter in a megacorp facility with attached lore. Doom Eternal is an aim-trainer with a story attached, much the same way a male anglerfish is attached to the female, fused on for reproductive convenience, vestigial, and visibly out of place. As Shroud put it: "[Doom Eternal] It's the best aim trainer." Which is to say, the game you [i]thought[/i] you were buying is the game you had already played. The game in front of you is a [b]different[/b] game, optimized for a different player experience, and graded against its own ambition rather than the predecessor's.

That's a taste objection, not a rejection; and taste objections grounded in game structure are legitimate. 

"I prefer Doom 2016" is a sentence a sane person can write. 

"Doom Eternal is bad because I prefer Doom 2016" is not. 

And the inability to tell those two sentences apart is, I am increasingly convinced, the single defining cognitive failure of the Steam gaming-reviewer ecosystem.

Which brings us to "Doc" Burford.

Burford's Medium[dot]com piece on Doom Eternal is the load-bearing citation in roughly half the negative Steam reviews of this game. It also reads like a film-studies undergraduate trying to extract a dissertation chapter out of being bad at fighting a [i]single[/i] enemy - the Marauder. The lexicon screams "auteur theory", but what he actually means is "I died a lot and didn't enjoy dying." If your critical foundation involves reaching for "auteur theory" to dismiss a game made by a five-hundred-person studio because you specifically cannot beat one miniboss, you are not doing criticism. You are Clark from [i]Good Will Hunting[/i]. You are buying the Nikola Tesla poster of game writing and hanging it next to your Flying Spaghetti Monster print (both of which you got from that idiot at [i]The Oatmeal[/i]) and telling people who don't know any better that this is what depth looks like.

And then, having done all that, Burford turns around and calls Destiny 2 the best game ever made. The Destiny 2 of "Eververse," of the gear sunsetting catastrophe, of the season-pass treadmill that consumes the franchise's good will (and the players' actual money) in real time. Yes, [i]that[/i] Destiny 2.

Pro-tip: If the theory you've imported from Burford into your review is the same theory that rates Destiny 2 above Doom Eternal, the problem is not Doom Eternal or Destiny 2. The problem is the theory. And that theory was downloaded from a Patreon mule with three followers and a cropped headshot. Get a different theory, or try playing the game without writing the review first.

review_english: There's a routine I engage in when I'm about to write a review. I play the game. Set it down for a few months (or in this case a few years) and then review it. The idea is to dodge the dopamine hit the flashy core mechanic, the bait-and-switch graphics and arrive at a verdict that has aged like a bottle of Domaine Leroy (1949). In the interim, I get to read takes in the reviews. Speaking of that... If a reviewer (paid critic or otherwise) marks down a game for a specific enemy and can't tell you which mechanic makes that enemy have a bad design, they're not reviewing the enemy. They're a couch and then complaining that it doesn't feel like the real thing. It's a self-own they're writing about themselves and calling it a review. Their citation to the effects of the mob on i their i gameplay, instead of the effects of the mob's mechanics on gameplay i in general i , is the dead giveaway that the opinion has no bearing on b you b . This situation applies singularly to the Marauder in Doom Eternal. Critics on Steam and on Medium dot com (presented in order of competency) have, since release, been going full pygmy marmoset, flinging their excrement of inadequacy at this miniboss i He breaks the run-and-gun. i i His shield prevents me from W-keying the game. i i I have to adjust my combat to take him into account. i The thing to recognize here is that these aren't critiques of an enemy, they're a confession by projection of the player, and the confession is that they shouldn't be publishing reviews anywhere. One should welcome an enemy who forces an adjustment, because that adjustment is the entire mechanism by which a combat game avoids feeling stale and programmatic. The Marauder is the moment Doom Eternal stops being a power fantasy (which, and I'll fight you on this, the series never was) and starts being a challenge. Marking it down for that moment is marking down the genre for being the genre. The irony is that the people marking the game down for the Marauder are missing that the Marauder b i is i b the game he's a microcosm of the entire design philosophy. The Marauder specifically, and Doom Eternal generally, demands you be on your A game 100 of the time. It is a fast game. It is a twitch game. It is a game that requires you to predict N 2 steps in advance. The keyboard is a Bösendorfer Imperial 290 and the game demands you play Beethoven's Klaviersonate Nr. 14 in cis-Moll, op. 27, Nr. 2 "Sonata quasi una fantasia", Beethoven-Werkverzeichnis-equivalent, third movement marked Presto agitato. It is, therefore, a game that rewards hypersituational awareness, fast reflexes, and a questionable dependency on second-hand-market Adderall. This is what elevates the game from a boomer shooter to Doom. It's the trust that id has for the gamers that it's been entertaining since Carmack decided that Commander Keen grew up, joined the marines, and got deployed to Mars to fix the UAC's mistake. But, having said that, Doom Eternal is not a game without complaints. I spent the first half of my playthrough convinced that id and Bethesda had lost their minds. The heavy emphasis on arenas. The resource-loop economy that herds you into a predetermined battle method if you don't want to scrounge for ammo mid-fight. The story drifting further and further from the series' founding mythos. None of this is Doom 2016. Doom 2016 was a shooter in a megacorp facility with attached lore. Doom Eternal is an aim-trainer with a story attached, much the same way a male anglerfish is attached to the female, fused on for reproductive convenience, vestigial, and visibly out of place. As Shroud put it " Doom Eternal It's the best aim trainer." Which is to say, the game you i thought i you were buying is the game you had already played. The game in front of you is a b different b game, optimized for a different player experience, and graded against its own ambition rather than the predecessor's. That's a taste objection, not a rejection and taste objections grounded in game structure are legitimate. "I prefer Doom 2016" is a sentence a sane person can write. "Doom Eternal is bad because I prefer Doom 2016" is not. And the inability to tell those two sentences apart is, I am increasingly convinced, the single defining cognitive failure of the Steam gaming-reviewer ecosystem. Which brings us to "Doc" Burford. Burford's Medium dot com piece on Doom Eternal is the load-bearing citation in roughly half the negative Steam reviews of this game. It also reads like a film-studies undergraduate trying to extract a dissertation chapter out of being bad at fighting a i single i enemy - the Marauder. review:
Well, this was surprising and a complete disappointment for me.

I'd go as far as to say this is single-handedly the most overrated and worst DOOM game I've played.
I've played 1, 2, 3, the 2016 reboot, and this. The 2016 reboot surprisingly flushes this one down the toilet. And I was at least able to complete 3, despite it not even really being a DOOM game.

In terms of positives, it's the usual modern DOOM things such as beautiful graphics, good musical storytelling, and decent environments. 

The main issues are glaringly obvious upon the first hour of gameplay. It's usually in the combat.

1. This game isn't a DOOM game like how 3 also wasn't a DOOM game. This game is a RPS (Rock Paper Scissors) and Resource Management game where you are fighting against the mechanics laid out before you rather than fighting against the monsters themselves. You are at constant war with the Ammo system, the Armor system, the 2 grenades you get, and the Chainsaw mechanic. This makes the game not necessarily more difficult, but instead more annoying and abrasive to deal with. On top of all of that, this still has the basically mandatory glory-kill system. You know, the worst part about 2016. Complete fail.

2. This is a tiring game, even more than Ultrakill (one of my favorite boomer-styled shooters). There is way too much going on in this game, in a bad way, with all of the upgrades, secrets, arenas, terrible "lore", and mechanics. It just straight up misses the point and charm of 2016. It's like they took all of the bad parts of the modern DOOM experience and quadrupled it. 

3. Climbing and movement options are clunky and poorly designed into the spaces of the levels. They really do make levels feel extremely linear. It's a good mechanic on paper, but in practice it's way too forced.

4. There is 0 freedom in the level design. "Secrets" and the bonus arenas are actually mandatory. The buffs and upgrades they all give you are way too valuable to actually warrant skipping them. This is bad for many reasons, but the main reason is that it creates a jarring gameplay experience where you go from frenetically dealing with the Ammo system in arenas, to exploring weirdly placed secrets. Again, tiring. 

5. There is 0 freedom in the combat experience. This is what really breaks the deal, in my opinion. DOOM is known for allowing you to play in the way that you, as the player, WANTS to play it. This game breaks this entirely by introducing RPS-style mechanics where specific weapons are used to break "weak-spots" to make kills more "efficient". This is so anti-DOOM that it's almost insane that people actually like this as a DOOM game to me.

It's not hard mechanically, like Ultrakill is, it's just tedious more than anything. This game feels like a miscalculation on all fronts. Too much focus on artificial difficulty, resource management, and, funnily of all, story and lore (in a DOOM game???).

I cannot recommend playing this game unless on HEAVY sale. Just play 2016 or boot up 2 with the Brutal DOOM mod for an actually fun experience.

Negative 2.5/5.

review:
A direct downgrade from Doom 2016 in nearly every way.

I was genuinely keen to finally get around to buying and playing this after finishing Doom 2016. I expected something fairly similar in terms of the menus, UI, and overall presentation. Instead, what I got was a bloated mess packed with corporate rubbish constantly trying to shove Bethesda’s branding and online nonsense in your face.

One of the most irritating parts is that the game forces you to make a Bethesda account before you can even properly start playing. Doom 2016 just threw you straight into the action without any of this corporate account nonsense getting in the way. They care more about pushing their ♥♥♥♥ down your throat than actually letting people enjoy the game.

When I launched the game for the first time the audio was completely stuffed and I had to spend about 15 minutes messing around with settings just to get sound working properly. After finally fixing that, I loaded into my save and the intro cinematic got skipped because it "wasn't a new save". I paused the game to go back to the main menu and could not find the option anywhere at all.

The whole experience felt scuffed and frustrating before I’d even properly started playing. Absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ of a game. Refunded.

review:
Overall:  4 out of 10

Compared to DOOM (2016), pretty much everything is lower in polish.  Way lower. 

It does try some new things as far as navigating the environment and new demons, but the results are a miss.  Mechanics that fall flat plus poor tuning, balancing and execution.  

Does run well on the Steam Deck and the graphics and sounds remain high quality.  That’s about the only positive.

All things considered, simply nowhere near as finely crafted as its predecessor, imho, no matter how pretty they made it.  And not worth buying.

And the mandatory Bethesda account/login?  Not interested.  But thanks for pushing that.

review:
[b]TLDR:[/b]
While the Doom Eternalis fun, at times, I dont like how the overall desing of the game feels compared to Doom 2016. I had a much more enjoyable time just shooting demons and not trying to solve jumping puzzles.

[b]Ammo / weapon feel:[/b]
For starters I imediatley noticed how there is no infinite ammo pistol. Which really sucks a ton early in the game before you get any ammo upgrades. 

I feel that the amount of ammo drops in the game can feel really sparce at times. Even running down to 0 ammo alot. Which really sucks when you mostly just want to be running and gunning. And instead having to focus on waiting for my chainsaw to recharge to get more ammo again.

[b]The map design is unclear:[/b]
Alot of the map design revolves around some kind of block that you have to puch and be able to jump up on. And there are many different areas where you have to use specific wall climb spots to get to higher points.
I've found several rooms that somewhat felt like a soft-lock but it turns out I was supposed to notice a random wall cling spot. Or there was other areas that I thought were locked because I couldnt punch underwater, but it turns out those walls I was supposed to dash into.

review: As a long-time fan of the series I can honestly say this is my least favourite game. I've played since the original 1993 release and played nearly every game. My favourite was Doom 3, I loved the horror elements. This is a constant spike of "Wow this is great, I'm fighting 50+ enemies" to "Okay, I've just spent over 20 minutes trying to figure out where to go or what to unlock, only for it to be a glitch where the game didn't trigger opening a gate. Then there's the "Oh ya, you need to do parkour with a couple jumps and dashes through 6 different poles and through a spiked timed gate." Then I try and fail like 10+ times and it just gets frustrating man... It's just not fun... Make it linear where I can just turn my brain off after work and shoot ♥♥♥♥... 2.5/10 I won't be buying anymore Doom games if they keep heading in this direction.

review:
Doom Eternal — Rage, Speed, and Relentless Carnage

Doom Eternal isn’t just a sequel; it’s a masterclass in aggressive first-person shooter design. Expanding upon the brutal foundations of its predecessor, the game transforms every encounter into a high-speed battle of movement, precision, and survival. Set against a universe consumed by demonic invasion, Doom Eternal pushes players into a relentless war where hesitation means death.

Combat feels faster, heavier, and far more strategic than before. The Doom Slayer is an unstoppable force, equipped with an arsenal designed to tear through enemies in spectacular fashion. Every mechanic — from Glory Kills to flame belches and chainsaw executions — feeds into a resource-driven combat loop that rewards aggression and constant motion. Each fight becomes a chaotic dance of bullets, explosions, and split-second decisions that demand mastery of both weapons and mobility.

Exploration adds surprising depth to the experience. Beyond the intense arenas, levels are packed with secrets, platforming challenges, hidden collectibles, and lore that flesh out the universe. The environments range from ruined Earth cities to nightmarish hellscapes and ancient alien worlds, each filled with intricate level design that encourages curiosity and experimentation.

Visually, Doom Eternal is stunning in its brutality. The vibrant color palette, detailed environments, and fluid animations make every encounter feel visceral and alive. Enemy designs are grotesque yet iconic, while the fast-paced movement system keeps gameplay incredibly smooth and satisfying. Accompanying the carnage is Mick Gordon’s thunderous metal soundtrack, perfectly amplifying the intensity and adrenaline of every battle.

[h3]Final Verdict:[/h3]
Doom Eternal elevates the modern shooter genre with unmatched speed, precision, and energy. Brutal yet rewarding, it delivers an exhilarating experience that constantly pushes players to improve, adapt, and embrace pure chaos.

“Rip and tear through the armies of Hell, master the rhythm of destruction, and become the ultimate force of unstoppable fury.”

Overall Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 10/10

review:
This is a 9/10 game, with phenomenal sound, graphics, and constant action.

So, why am I not recommending it?

I think that this game is an acquired taste, one which I sadly can't share with the other fans. The thing is that my dislike of this game is the exact opposite amount of love I had for Doom 2016, a game, which in my opinion, is a modern classic in the FPS genre.

I'm not exactly sure what must have happened to take the devs this way, but the gameplay itself just doesn't agree with me. The action, is fantastic. However, the way it ensues is anything but. You see, instead of the simplicity of 2016, the game commits the sin of every other sequel in the late 2010/2020s, which is adding gameplay on top of gameplay systems for no rhyme or reason. While some might like the additional challenge of having to swap between differently colored weapons, ammo, upgrades, chainsaw/flamethrower, grenades and the lot, it's a bit too much. For a shooter, this game uses one too many buttons on the keyboard and mouse.

The fact that you have to switch your weapons (with a measly amount of ammo I might add) around every 5 seconds to target small enemy weakpoints for massive damage (ehm), while there's a ton of frenetic action happening around the player is just rage inducing. Which would be cool, but it's just tiresome, as opposed to being fun. In fact, the game is full of tutorials up until the last level, which is crazy. Oh but you do need that guidance when you face enemies like the marauders, who not only need to be targeted during their extremely blink and you miss it phases to kill, but they also take just so much time to take down that it just made me disappointed in their design, more than anything. 

Not to mention the platforming. Why did Doom need to introduce Mario-like platforming into the game? It's okay, but thanks to it, the levels make no logical sense. While 2016 had a pretty straightforward level design, this one doesn't. The levels don't flow well...perhaps apart from Mars core, that one was cool.

Ultimately, this game (for me) was just too tedious to play, which is why I'm not recommending it. If you like the challenge, go ahead, but those looking for a bit of power fantasy, this just ain't it.

UPDATE: The DLC is just all the bad parts of Eternal turned up to 11—including the story, which has become a bunch of absolute nonsense.
review_clean: This is a 9 10 game, with phenomenal sound, graphics, and constant action. So, why am I not recommending it? I think that this game is an acquired taste, one which I sadly can't share with the other fans. The thing is that my dislike of this game is the exact opposite amount of love I had for Doom 2016, a game, which in my opinion, is a modern classic in the FPS genre. I'm not exactly sure what must have happened to take the devs this way, but the gameplay itself just doesn't agree with me. The action, is fantastic. However, the way it ensues is anything but. You see, instead of the simplicity of 2016, the game commits the sin of every other sequel in the late 2010 2020s, which is adding gameplay on top of gameplay systems for no rhyme or reason. While some might like the additional challenge of having to swap between differently colored weapons, ammo, upgrades, chainsaw flamethrower, grenades and the lot, it's a bit too much. For a shooter, this game uses one too many buttons on the keyboard and mouse. The fact that you have to switch your weapons (with a measly amount of ammo I might add) around every 5 seconds to target small enemy weakpoints for massive damage (ehm), while there's a ton of frenetic action happening around the player is just rage inducing. Which would be cool, but it's just tiresome, as opposed to being fun. In fact, the game is full of tutorials up until the last level, which is crazy. Oh but you do need that guidance when you face enemies like the marauders, who not only need to be targeted during their extremely blink and you miss it phases to kill, but they also take just so much time to take down that it just made me disappointed in their design, more than anything. Not to mention the platforming. Why did Doom need to introduce Mario-like platforming into the game? It's okay, but thanks to it, the levels make no logical sense. While 2016 had a pretty straightforward level design, this one doesn't. The levels don't flow well...perhaps apart from Mars core, that one was cool. Ultimately, this game (for me) was just too tedious to play, which is why I'm not recommending it. If you like the challenge, go ahead, but those looking for a bit of power fantasy, this just ain't it. review:
어느 게임의 후속작을 사는 이유 자체가 전작 게임성의 연장선을 추구하는거지 전작과 이질적인 무언가를 바란다면 애초에 다른 게임을 샀을거라고 생각함
완성도가 낮다는 얘기가 아님. 둠 이터널은 2016년 둠 리부트의 후속작이지만 전작과는 상당히 이질적인 게임임

취향을 내려놓고 객관적으로 보자면 둠 리부트는 데미지를 뻥튀기할수 있는 버그성 기술들(BFG쏘고 무기 교체창 띄워서 시간 느리게 하면 딜 몇배로 들어간다던가)을 제외하고서라도 밸런스적인 측면에서는 완벽한 게임은 아니었음.

다만 밸런스가 무너졌다고 해서 재미가 없었냐 하면 그건 아님. 흐름이 끊기지 않고 악마들을 시원하게 갈아버리는 재미는 확실한데다 멀티플레이가 핵심인 게임도 아니고 무기간 밸런스가 안 맞는다는게 이런 경험에 방해가 되는건 아님.

반면 둠 이터널은 디렉터부터가 이런 게임성을 단점으로 생각한건지 체력, 탄약, 갑옷의 배치를 줄이고 자원을 획득하기 위해선 글로리킬, 전기톱, 화염방사기 등의 요소들을 활용하게 하는 식으로 전략적으로 자원 관리를 하게 만들고 머라우더로 대표되는 특정 기믹을 수행해야 잡을 수 있는 몹, 혹은 아크바일처럼 우선적으로 처리해야 하는 적들을 배치함

문제는 이런 변화가 전작하곤 다르게 플레이어의 플레이스타일을 강제하게 만든다는거임. 머라우더는 처음 중간 보스로 등장한 이후엔 일반 적으로 등장하는데 설계 자체가 특정 타이밍에 맞춰 특정 무기군을 사용하도록 플레이어에게 강요하기 때문에  흐름을 끊어먹는다고 여겨질 정도임. 

그리고 확실히 단점이라고 말할 수 있는건 이렇게 전작보다 빡빡한 시스템과 구성을 만들어놓고선 플레이어한테 게임플레이를 통해서, 플레이어에게 기믹이나 시스템을 보여주고 학습시켜서 수행하도록 유도하는 식의 학습곡선을 쌓는 시도조차 안하고 게임 진행 중간중간에 새로운 무언가가 나올때마다 메시지박스 띄워서 대놓고 얘는 어떻게 잡아야한다고 모든 공략을 알려줘야한다는거부터가 구조적인 실패라고 생각함

컴뱃퍼즐이니 뭐니 하면서 개발자가 의도한 밸런스라고 하는데 애초에 빡빡한 자원관리와 몹에 상성같은게 있는 게임을 하고싶었으면 뭐 소울라이크라던가 시스템쇼크마냥 자원관리와 상성 혹은 패턴 공략이 핵심이 되는 게임을 하러가지 않았을까

내 취향이 아니라고 못 만들었다는 건 아니라 일단 해보고 판단하라고 추천을 남김. 하지만 게임성이 전작보다도 취향을 더 탈거라고 생각하고 분명 이번작이 완성도가 더 높다고들 하지만 정작 실제 플레이어의 의견이 반영되는 스팀에서의  평가가 전작은 압도적으로 긍정적이고 후속작은 매우 긍정적의 차이가 있다는것부터가 게임성이 더 취향이 갈리게 변한 것과 그로 인한 전작을 플레이한 유저의 기대와의 괴리 때문이 아닌가 생각함

review_english: The reason for buying a sequel to a game is to pursue an extension of the previous game's gameplay. If you want something different from the previous game, you should have bought a different game in the first place. This does not mean that the level of completion is low. Doom Eternal is a sequel to the 2016 Doom Reboot, but it is a very different game from the previous work. If you put aside your tastes and look at it objectively, Doom Reboot was not a perfect game in terms of balance, even excluding buggy techniques that can increase damage (such as shooting BFGs and opening the weapon change window to slow down time, doubling the damage). However, just because the balance was off doesn't mean it wasn't fun. It's definitely fun to grind demons without breaking the flow, and the fact that it's not a game with multiplayer at its core and the lack of balance between weapons doesn't hinder this experience. On the other hand, Doom Eternal, perhaps because the director considered this to be a drawback, reduces the deployment of health, ammunition, and armor, and makes resource management strategically by requiring the use of elements such as glory kills, chainsaws, and flamethrowers to acquire resources. It also places mobs that can be caught only by performing a specific gimmick, such as the Marauder, or enemies that must be dealt with first, such as Archvile. The problem is that these changes force the player's playstyle to be different from previous games. The Marauder first appears as a mid-boss and then appears generally, but the design itself forces the player to use a specific weapon group at a specific timing, so it can be considered as breaking the flow. And what can definitely be said to be a disadvantage is that, after creating a system and composition that is tighter than the previous work, they do not even try to build a learning curve by showing the player the gimmick or system through gameplay and encouraging them to learn and perform it. I think it is a structural failure that every time something new appears in the middle of the game, they have to pop up a message box and tell them all the strategies and how to catch it. It is a combat puzzle and all. It's called balance, but if I wanted to play a game with tight resource management and compatibility with mobs in the first place, I wouldn't have gone for a game like Soullike or System Shock where resource management and compatibility or pattern strategy are the key. I'm not saying I couldn't make it just because it's not my taste, but I'm leaving a recommendation for you to try it first and judge for yourself. However, I think that the game quality will be more popular than the previous work, and it is said that this work has a higher level of completion. However, in fact, the evaluation on Steam, which reflects the opinions of actual players, is overwhelmingly positive for the previous game and the very positive difference for the sequel. I think that this is due to the fact that the game quality has changed to become more diverse in taste and the resulting gap between the expectations of users who played the previous work.

review:
Провів у DOOM Eternal близько 15 годин і можу сказати, що це один із найкращих шутерів, у які я коли-небудь грав. Гра з перших хвилин задає шалений темп і не відпускає до самого кінця. Тут практично немає місця для перепочинку — ти постійно рухаєшся, стрибаєш, ухиляєшся від атак та знищуєш цілі орди демонів.

Найбільше мене вразив геймплей. На відміну від багатьох сучасних шутерів, DOOM Eternal змушує використовувати весь арсенал зброї та здібностей. Неможливо пройти гру, використовуючи лише одну улюблену гармату. Кожен тип ворогів має свої слабкі місця, тому доводиться швидко приймати рішення та постійно адаптуватися до ситуації на полі бою.

Бойова система відчувається неймовірно динамічною. Коли починаєш розуміти всі механіки, кожна битва перетворюється на справжній танець смерті, де одна помилка може коштувати життя. Особливе задоволення приносить поєднання різних видів зброї, бензопили, вогнемета та добивань, які не лише виглядають ефектно, а й допомагають отримувати боєприпаси, броню та здоров'я.

Окремо варто похвалити саундтрек. Важкий метал ідеально підходить до того, що відбувається на екрані, і робить кожен бій ще більш епічним. Графіка також виглядає чудово: локації різноманітні, вороги деталізовані, а спецефекти додають видовищності.

Сподобалося й те, що гра не затягує проходження штучно. Майже кожна місія пропонує нові випробування, механіки або типи ворогів. Секрети та колекційні предмети мотивують уважно досліджувати рівні, а система прокачування дозволяє поступово ставати ще сильнішим.

З мінусів можу відзначити, що гра досить вимоглива до реакції та уваги. Новачкам серії або тим, хто звик до повільніших шутерів, перші години можуть здатися складними. Також сюжет тут явно не є головною причиною грати, хоча він непогано доповнює загальну атмосферу.

Загалом DOOM Eternal став для мене прикладом того, яким має бути сучасний шутер. Швидкий, видовищний, складний і неймовірно захопливий. За 15 годин гра подарувала величезну кількість адреналіну та яскравих моментів, і я точно можу рекомендувати її всім любителям екшенів та шутерів від першої особи.

Однозначно рекомендую.

review_english: I spent about 15 hours in DOOM Eternal and I can say that it is one of the best shooters I have ever played. The game sets a frantic pace from the first minutes and does not let go until the very end. There is practically no place to rest here, you are constantly moving, jumping, dodging attacks and destroying entire hordes of demons. The gameplay impressed me the most. Unlike many modern shooters, DOOM Eternal forces you to use the entire arsenal of weapons and abilities. It is impossible to complete the game using only one favorite gun. Each type of enemy has its weaknesses, so you have to make quick decisions and constantly adapt to the situation on the battlefield. The combat system feels incredibly dynamic. When you start to understand all the mechanics, every battle turns into a real dance of death, where one mistake can cost you a life. The combination of different types of weapons, chainsaws, flamethrowers and finishers, which not only look spectacular, but also help you get ammunition, armor and health, brings special pleasure. The soundtrack should be praised separately. The heavy metal perfectly matches what's happening on screen and makes every fight even more epic. The graphics also look great, the locations are varied, the enemies are detailed, and the special effects add to the spectacle. I also liked the fact that the game does not delay the passage artificially. Almost every mission offers new challenges, mechanics, or enemy types. Secrets and collectibles motivate you to explore the levels carefully, and the leveling system allows you to gradually become even stronger. Among the minuses, I can note that the game is quite demanding in terms of reaction and attention. Those new to the series or those used to slower shooters may find the first few hours difficult. Also, the plot here is clearly not the main reason to play, although it nicely complements the general atmosphere. In general, DOOM Eternal became for me an example of what a modern shooter should be. Fast, spectacular, complex and incredibly exciting. In 15 hours, the game gave a huge amount of adrenaline and bright moments, and I can definitely recommend it to all fans of action and first-person shooters. Definitely recommend.

review:
Doom Eternal is one of the best arcade shooters ever made. The combat is fast, aggressive, and constantly forces you to think on the move. Every weapon has a purpose, every demon is a priority target, and the resource system (chainsaw for ammo, glory kills for health, flame belch for armor) turns every fight into a high-speed puzzle. When everything clicks, few shooters can match its intensity.

The movement is excellent, with dashes, monkey bars, and platforming sections making arenas feel dynamic rather than flat shooting galleries. The soundtrack, visuals, and overall feeling of power make every battle memorable.

However, the game isn't perfect. One of the biggest issues is how certain boss-type enemies become regular enemies later in the game. The Marauder is a great example. As a boss encounter, he is unique and challenging, requiring patience, timing, and understanding of his attack patterns. But once the game starts throwing Marauders into regular combat encounters alongside multiple other demons, the fight can become frustrating rather than fun.

The Marauder's mechanics feel designed for a duel: he blocks attacks from long range, punishes you at close range, summons his wolf, and only opens himself for a brief counterattack window. In a 1v1 fight, this works well. In a crowded arena filled with other demons, it can feel like the game is asking you to stop playing aggressively and focus on one specific enemy's rules while everything else attacks you.

The same criticism applies to some other boss-inspired enemies that lose part of what made them special once they become standard opponents. What felt like a memorable encounter starts feeling like an obstacle repeatedly inserted into normal gameplay.

Despite that, Doom Eternal remains an outstanding shooter. Its combat system is deep, rewarding, and unlike almost anything else on the market. The few frustrating enemy designs don't outweigh the incredible gameplay loop.

Final Score: 8.5/10

review:
Итак, перепрошёл всю трилогию дум подряд и составилось мнение по всем 3-м частям серии. Проходил всё на кошмаре. Итак, по делу.
    Визуал. Он стал в разы разнообразнее в сравнении с doom 2016 и по сути повторяется только 2 раза и это 2 уровня Некровол. Дизайны врагов и оружия больше стали приближены к Дум 2, из за чего есть небольшая мультяшность. Ах да, мультяшность... Честно, я не знаю почему все так прикопались к этим цветным патронам, вообще не замечаешь этого в пылу боя. А вот звёздочки над врагами и специальный звук при оглушении уже перебор как по мне, но общее впечатление об игре не портит, потому что я захожу в Дум за геймплеем.
    Сюжет пох, кто нибудь другой расскажет.
    Геймплей в игре стал лучше в сравнении с doom 2016, он наиболее сбалансирован, бесполезных оружий нету, как и гранат, теперь бесполезных модулей тоже нет, а от рун имбовых избавились, что очень круто. Теперь бензопилой надо пользоваться, но любители поиграть только с одним оружием обломались, потому чо теперь патронов на супердробовик не 60, а всего лишь 24, поэтому теперь нужно менять оружие постоянно (чему обучают в подсказке) и учить комбухи, если хочешь эффективно уничтожать демонов. Ты либо принимаешь эти правила и учишься играть, либо ставишь низкую сложность))). На уровнях появились балки для раскачивания, а Палач обзавёлся крюком на супердробовик, из за чего передвигаешься в разы быстрее, тем более с дешами, а сами уровни очень даже хорошие и не раздражают совсем. Боссфайты в игре нормальные: 1-й и 2-й боссфайты обычные, вполне радуют, 3-й слишком лёгкий, а 4-й чуть-чуть бесит своим хаосом на арене, но терпимо всё ещё. А вот в длс... В первом длс вполне прикольный боссфайт получился, а вот во втором длс он отвратительный. Просто всё самое худшее в нём собрали: вампиризм, постоянный спавн врагов, на которых нужно тратить молот и во время этого он обязательно на тебя нападёт со спины, рандомность его атак, постоянно в тебя бросает всякими бомбами. В думе 2016 года в целом боссфайты все были хорошими, по край не мере точно не раздражали и не были скучными. 
   Музыка в игре тоже очень хороша. В этот раз запоминающихся треков было на порядок больше, но прям разье bHых не было (хотя Meathook был в духе саунда из doom 2016). Главное хорошо работает на геймплей. 
    Игру конечно советую и я пока что считаю эту часть серии лучшей. Doom eternal имееть буквально идеальный геймплей, который улучшить невозможно, что и доказали ДЛС, просто ограничив игроков в выборе модулей на пушки. Именно она поменяла, по сути, мою жизнь. По край не мере она определила мой стиль геймплея и я в неё играл довольно много.

review_english: So, I replayed the entire trilogy of dooms in a row and formed an opinion on all 3 parts of the series. I went through everything like a nightmare. So, let's get to the point. Visual. It has become many times more diverse compared to doom 2016 and is essentially repeated only 2 times and these are 2 levels of Nekrovol. The designs of enemies and weapons have become closer to Doom 2, which is why there is a slight cartoonish look. Oh yes, the cartoonishness... Honestly, I don’t know why everyone is so obsessed with these colored cartridges, you don’t even notice it in the heat of battle. But the stars above the enemies and the special sound when stunned are already too much for me, but they don’t spoil the overall impression of the game, because I go to Doom for the gameplay. Fuck the plot, someone else will tell it. The gameplay in the game has become better compared to doom 2016, it is more balanced, there are no useless weapons, like grenades, now there are no useless modules either, and they got rid of the imbec runes, which is very cool. Now you need to use a chainsaw, but those who like to play with only one weapon are screwed, because now there are not 60 cartridges for a super shotgun, but only 24, so now you need to change weapons constantly (which is taught in the tooltip) and learn kombukhi if you want to effectively destroy demons. You either accept these rules and learn to play, or set the difficulty to low))). Beams for swinging appeared at the levels, and the Executioner acquired a hook for a super shotgun, which is why you move many times faster, especially with dashes, and the levels themselves are very good and not annoying at all. The bossfights in the game are normal, the 1st and 2nd bossfights are normal, quite enjoyable, the 3rd is too easy, and the 4th is a little annoying with its chaos in the arena, but still tolerable. But in the DLC... In the first DLC the boss fight turned out to be quite cool, but in the second DLC it is disgusting. It’s just that all the worst things in him were collected by vampirism, the constant spawning of enemies on whom you need to spend a hammer and during this he will definitely attack you from behind, the randomness of his attacks, he constantly throws all sorts of bombs at you. In the Duma of 2016, in general, the boss fights were all good, at least certainly not annoying or boring. The music in the game is also very good. This time there were an order of magnitude more memorable tracks, but there were no really great ones (although Meathook was in the spirit of the sound from doom 2016). The main thing is that it works well for gameplay. I certainly recommend the game and I still think this part of the series is the best. Doom eternal has literally perfect gameplay that cannot be improved, as the DLC proved, simply by limiting players in the choice of modules for guns. She was the one who essentially changed my life. At the very least, it determined my gameplay style and I played it quite a lot.

review:
Tu lucha es ETERNA.


Una increíble y sangrienta secuela, luego de no tan convencido que salí de Doom 2016.
Lograron pulir con mucho cariño la esencia original y clásica.
Diseño de niveles estratégicos y acomodados tanto para ti como para los enemigos que te acechan.
Armas y habilidades mejor desarrolladas para darte el mayor gusto y placer a la hora de destrozar demonios.
Y como no, diseño de historia y desarrollo del UNF espectacular.

En cuanto a sus DLC(The ancient gods, me he quedado algo disgustado al ser tan cortos y por alguna razón que lo hicieran en dos partes separadas.
Pero lograron darle un buen desenlace a la historia como tal.
Al igual como en la entrega anterior, el platino no fue del todo legal debido a su multijugador muerto.

Sin duda un juego que disfruté mucho por sus experiencias únicas en combate y dificultad, banda sonora brutal de parte de Mick Gordon y aspecto gráfico difícil de replicar.

review_english: Your fight is ETERNAL. An incredible and bloody sequel, after not being so convinced that I left Doom 2016. They managed to lovingly polish the original and classic essence. Design of strategic and comfortable levels for both you and the enemies that stalk you. Better developed weapons and skills to give you the greatest pleasure and pleasure when destroying demons. And of course, the history design and development of the spectacular UNF. As for their DLC (The ancient gods), I was somewhat disappointed that they were so short and for some reason that they did it in two separate parts. But they managed to give a good ending to the story as such. As in the previous installment, the platinum was not completely legal due to its dead multiplayer. Without a doubt a game that I enjoyed a lot for its unique experiences in combat and difficulty, brutal soundtrack by Mick Gordon and graphical aspect that is difficult to replicate.

review:
Positiv:
----------
+ wirkt technisch in vielerlei Hinsicht imposant & ausgereift
+ hervorragende Animationen & Effekte
+ sehr stimmiges Art-Design
+ bombastische Soundkulisse
+ dynamisch implementierter, äußerst stimmiger Soundtrack
+ insgesamt eine absolut phänomenale Inszenierung
+ unerwartet ausgeklügelte(s) Lore & Worldbuildung
+ äußerst umfangreicher Lore-Kodex
+ enorm dichte Oldschool-Atmosphäre
+ komplexe und dennoch einfach zu erlernende Bedienung
+ sechs verschiedene Schwierigkeitsgrade
+ unglaublich krasses Pacing
+ vorbildliche Lernkurve
+ sehr deftiges & wunderbar kompromissloses Gunplay
+ hervorragend designte Waffen
+ mit die intensivsten Arena-Fights, die ich bisher gesehen habe
+ spitzenmäßig inszenierte Gegner…
+ …die vor allem in gemischten Gruppen brandgefährlich & herrlich unnachgiebig agieren
+ cleveres, eindrucksvolles & thematisch abwechslungsreiches Leveldesign
+ taktisch spannendes Ressourcenmanagement
+ gleichermaßen spaßige wie wichtige Jagd nach Secrets
+ einfallsreiche Upgrade-Mechaniken
+ überraschend smarte & gut funktionierende Gameplay-Puzzles
+ viele Easter Eggs & Referenzen zu id Software
+ haufenweise Endgame-Content & viele Challenges…
+ ...was sich sehr gut auf den Wiederspielwert auswirkt
+ läuft bugfrei & stabil auf meinem System
+ DOOM 1 & 2 ingame spielbar

Negativ:
----------
- die anfängliche Munitionsknappheit wirkt zunächst verwirrend & störend
- Tutorials, Tutorials, Tutorials
- knapp ein halbes Dutzend Ingame-Währungen für die Upgrade-Mechaniken
- wirkt storytechnisch anfangs recht unverständlich & wirr…
- ...und ohne das Einsammeln der teils versteckten Lore-Bits verstärkt sich dieser Effekt noch
- einige aufgesetzt wirkende Belohnungen (Skins, Emotes)

[b]DOOM 2016 hat zwei Dinge geschafft: Es hat das DOOM-Franchise wiederbelebt und dabei gleichzeitig das seinerzeit selbst erschaffene Genre mit einem Schlag neu definiert. So etwas passiert nur äußerst selten in der Gaming-Welt, und als ein Nachfolger angekündigt wurde, waren die Erwartungen immens. Die Entwickler haben aber richtig krass abgeliefert, denn „DOOM Eternal“ ist ein brutales, cleveres und zugleich verspieltes Meisterwerk, dass seinen exzellenten Vorgänger in so gut wie allen Bereichen übertrifft – etwas anderes zu behaupten entspräche nicht der Wahrheit.[/b]

Eternal bietet mit die intensivsten Arena-Fights, die ich je in einem Singleplayer-Shooter erlebt habe. Es lässt sich nur schwer beschreiben, wie ausgeklügelt und… „formvollendet“ die Gameplay-Mechaniken ineinandergreifen und so ein Erlebnis erschaffen wird, dass seinesgleichen im Genre sucht. Zugegebenermaßen braucht Eternal eine Weile, um in Fahrt zu kommen und in den ersten Leveln wird zwar angedeutet, wohin die Reise in Sachen Leveldesign und Arena-Fights gehen wird, man wird aber auch mit einer streng limitierten Munitionskapazität sowie mit Jump-Puzzles konfrontiert, und es will einem zunächst nicht so richtig in die Birne, was beides überhaupt in einem DOOM-Spiel zu suchen hat. Das Ganze ändert sich danach aber recht zügig und durch zunehmende Waffen- und Gegner-Vielfalt sowie durch Hilfe von Upgrades, die unter anderem die Munitionsgrenze erhöhen, wird die Intensität massiv nach oben geschraubt. Jeder Arena-Kampf wird dann zu einem berauschenden Spektakel, in denen Kaum Zeit zum Luftholen bleibt. Schon der dritte von sechs Schwierigkeitsgraden ist dabei knallhart und um Erfolg zu haben, ist es unumgänglich, alle zur Verfügung stehenden Mechaniken einzusetzen. Um z.B. an Rüstungsteile zu kommen, muss man Gegner mit dem Schulter-Flammenwerfer in Brand setzen, bevor man sie tötet, Lebensenergie bekommt man durch Glory Kills und Munition bekommt man durch das Zersägen von Gegnern mit der Kettensäge. Statt also wie in DOOM 2016 klassisch auf Health- und Munitions-Pickups zu setzen, fordert Eternal ein grundlegendes umdenken und besonderes Engagement vom Spieler, was sich in der Praxis aber äußerst unterhaltsam spielt, da man ständig dazu genötigt wird, in Bewegung zu bleiben und Gegner unter Druck zu setzen. Die Schauplätze, die brandgefährlichen Gegner sowie auch die herrlich bescheuerten Waffen wurden dementsprechend designt und es ist ein absolut atemberaubendes Gefühl, sich durch die Arenen zu schwingen und dabei die Grenzen des Machbaren auszuloten. Und schließlich das Leveldesign, mit seinen absolut phänomenalen Set Pieces, der markanten Stilrichtung (Masters of the Universe lässt grüßen!) sowie auch den etwas kontrovers diskutierten Jump-Puzzles, funktioniert meiner Meinung nach so gut und schlüssig, dass ich hier kaum echte Kritikpunkte anbringen kann.

Typisch DOOM gibt’s wieder richtig was auf die Ohren. Der Soundtrack geht richtig steil und trägt enorm zur Atmosphäre bei, während die kraftvollen Soundeffekte den gorelastigen Glory Kills und den deftig klingenden Waffen ein fast schon greifbares Gefühl verleihen, sodass man mit einer ordentlichen Soundanlage den Nachbarn problemlos den Schlaf rauben könnte, wenn man denn wollte. Aber nicht nur das, denn auch grafisch hat Eternal einiges zu bieten. Was hier mit der id Tech 7 umgesetzt wurde, setzt zwar keine neuen Maßstäbe, funktioniert aber dennoch so gut und sieht so gut aus, dass es eine besondere Erwähnung verdient: Zahlreiche Grafikeinstellungen, stabile Framerates auch auf älterer Hardware, butterweiche Animationen, hübsche Effekte und Shader sowie einiges mehr spricht dafür, dass im Entwicklerteam die richtigen Leute an den richtigen Positionen sitzen, was heutzutage absolut nicht mehr selbstverständlich ist.

Und selbst Storytelling und Worldbuilding überraschen und überzeugen auf eine ganz eigene Art, auch wenn das für viele DOOM-Fans gar nicht so relevant sein dürfte. Die Mühe, die sich hier gemacht wurde, mithilfe von Cutscenes, Charakteren sowie einer zugegebenermaßen ziemlich bescheuerten, sehr protzigen, aber auch nachvollziehbaren Lore einen unterhaltsamen erzählerischen Rahmen zu liefern, ist für DOOM-Verhältnisse durchaus bemerkenswert. Zweifellos habe ich im Vorwege eine bestimmte Erwartungshaltung gehabt, aber das DOOM auf einmal Fraktionen bekommt, darunter technologisch hochentwickelte Aliens, die unter anderem Himmel und Hölle repräsentieren - damit hab ich ganz sicher nicht gerechnet. Ähnlich wie bei den Jump-Puzzles kann man natürlich auch hier jetzt die Nase rümpfen, dass sich das DOOM-Franchise in diese Richtung bewegt, aber hey, die Essenz des Spiels bleibt nach wie vor, mit völlig übertriebenen Waffen solange auf grauenhafte Höllenbiester zu schießen, bis sich nichts mehr bewegt oder zwischendurch halt mal einen Schalter zu drücken. That’s it. Außerdem ist man eh selber schuld, wenn man DOOM ernster nimmt, als DOOM sich selbst, was ernstgemeintes Gatekeeping noch um einiges dümmer macht, als es eh schon ist, wenn ich so drüber nachdenke. Wie auch immer, ich selbst habe durchaus meine Freude an den ganzen Easter Eggs, Plüschfiguren und Schallplatten gehabt (die Album-Artworks...Hammer). Das einzige Problem, dass ich mit dem Storytelling habe, ist, dass man für die Mehrheit der Spielzeit im Unklaren bleibt, was eigentlich abgeht. Und obwohl klar sein sollte, dass Eternal storytechnisch auf den Ereignissen von DOOM 2016 aufbaut, so lassen sich die Geschichte des Slayers sowie das Wie und Warum der Ereignisse und der Fraktionen nur durch ein akribisches Einsammeln der überall verstreuten Lore-Bits vollends nachvollziehen, was durchaus kritikwürdig ist, sich andererseits aber auch so gut wie gar nicht auf den Gameplay-Loop auswirkt.

Wenn man noch etwas kritisieren wollte, dann, dass es für jeden Mist eine Hilfestellung gibt. Ist es wirklich nötig, dass man für jeden neuen Gegner oder jede neue Waffe direkt die Schwachpunkte bzw. Strategien präsentiert bekommt?! Wo bleibt da die Spannung und die Entdeckerfreude?! Zum Glück lassen sich diese anfänglich [u]ständig[/u] aufpoppenden Tutorials in den Optionen abschalten.

Den Multiplayer-Modus habe ich nicht ausprobiert.

9,5/10

Ähnlich wie bei den Jump-Puzzles kann man natürlich auch hier jetzt die Nase rümpfen, dass sich das DOOM-Franchise in diese Richtung bewegt, aber hey, die Essenz des Spiels bleibt nach wie vor, mit völlig übertriebenen Waffen solange auf grauenhafte Höllenbiester zu schießen, bis sich nichts mehr bewegt oder zwischendurch halt mal einen Schalter zu drücken. That s it. Außerdem ist man eh selber schuld, wenn man DOOM ernster nimmt, als DOOM sich selbst, was ernstgemeintes Gatekeeping noch um einiges dümmer macht, als es eh schon ist, wenn ich so drüber nachdenke. Wie auch immer, ich selbst habe durchaus meine Freude an den ganzen Easter Eggs, Plüschfiguren und Schallplatten gehabt (die Album-Artworks...Hammer). Das einzige Problem, dass ich mit dem Storytelling habe, ist, dass man für die Mehrheit der Spielzeit im Unklaren bleibt, was eigentlich abgeht. Und obwohl klar sein sollte, dass Eternal storytechnisch auf den Ereignissen von DOOM 2016 aufbaut, so lassen sich die Geschichte des Slayers sowie das Wie und Warum der Ereignisse und der Fraktionen nur durch ein akribisches Einsammeln der überall verstreuten Lore-Bits vollends nachvollziehen, was durchaus kritikwürdig ist, sich andererseits aber auch so gut wie gar nicht auf den Gameplay-Loop auswirkt. Wenn man noch etwas kritisieren wollte, dann, dass es für jeden Mist eine Hilfestellung gibt. Ist es wirklich nötig, dass man für jeden neuen Gegner oder jede neue Waffe direkt die Schwachpunkte bzw. Strategien präsentiert bekommt?! Wo bleibt da die Spannung und die Entdeckerfreude?! Zum Glück lassen sich diese anfänglich u ständig u aufpoppenden Tutorials in den Optionen abschalten. Den Multiplayer-Modus habe ich nicht ausprobiert. 9,5 10
review_english: Positive ---------- seems technically impressive in many respects, excellent animations, effects, very harmonious art design, bombastic soundscape, dynamically implemented, extremely harmonious soundtrack, overall, an absolutely phenomenal production, unexpectedly sophisticated lore, worldbuilding, extremely extensive lore code, extremely dense old school atmosphere, complex and yet easy to learn operation, six different levels of difficulty, incredibly awesome pacing, exemplary learning curve hearty, wonderfully uncompromising gunplay, excellently designed weapons with the most intense arena fights I've seen so far, superbly staged opponents who act extremely dangerously, wonderfully unyielding, especially in mixed groups, clever, impressive, thematically varied level design, tactically exciting resource management, equally fun and important hunt for secrets, imaginative upgrade mechanics, surprisingly smart, well-functioning gameplay puzzles, lots of Easter eggs references to id Software tons of endgame content lots of challenges ...which has a very good effect on the replay value runs bug-free and stable on my system DOOM 1 2 can be played in-game Negative ---------- - the initial shortage of ammunition seems confusingly disturbing at first - tutorials, tutorials, tutorials - almost half a dozen in-game currencies for the upgrade mechanics - in terms of the story it initially seems quite incomprehensibly confusing - ...and without collecting the partly hidden ones Lore bits make this effect even stronger - some seemingly fake rewards (skins, emotes). It's extremely rare for something like this to happen in the gaming world, and when a sequel was announced, expectations were immense. But the developers have really delivered, because DOOM Eternal is a brutal, clever and at the same time playful masterpiece that surpasses its excellent predecessor in almost all areas. To say otherwise would not be the truth. b Eternal offers some of the most intense arena fights I have ever experienced in a single player shooter. It is difficult to describe how sophisticated and perfectly formed the gameplay mechanics are, creating an experience that is unparalleled in the genre. Admittedly, Eternal takes a while to get going and the first few levels hint at where the journey is going in terms of level design and arena fights, but you are also confronted with a strictly limited ammunition capacity and jump puzzles, and at first you don't really understand what both of these have to do with a DOOM game. However, the whole thing changes quite quickly after that and the intensity is massively increased due to the increasing variety of weapons and enemies as well as the help of upgrades that, among other things, increase the ammunition limit. Every arena fight then becomes an exhilarating spectacle in which there is hardly any time to breathe. Even the third of six levels of difficulty is extremely tough and in order to be successful it is essential to use all available mechanics. For example, to get armor pieces, you have to set enemies on fire with the shoulder flamethrower before killing them, you get life energy through glory kills, and you get ammunition by sawing enemies with the chainsaw. Instead of relying on health and ammunition pickups like in DOOM 2016, Eternal demands a fundamental rethink and special commitment from the player, which in practice is extremely entertaining because you are constantly forced to keep moving and put opponents under pressure. The locations, the extremely dangerous opponents and the wonderfully stupid weapons were designed accordingly and it is an absolutely breathtaking feeling to swing through the arenas and explore the limits of what is possible. And finally the level design, with its absolutely phenomenal set pieces, the striking style (greetings from Masters of the Universe!) as well as the somewhat controversial jump puzzles, works so well and coherently in my opinion that I can hardly make any real criticisms here. Typically DOOM, it's really exciting. The soundtrack really packs a punch and adds a lot to the atmosphere, while the powerful sound effects give the gore-heavy glory kills and beefy-sounding weapons an almost tangible feel, so with a decent sound system you could easily keep the neighbors awake if you wanted to. A Not only that, Eternal also has a lot to offer graphically. What was implemented here with the id Tech 7 doesn't set new standards, but it still works so well and looks so good that it deserves a special mention. Numerous graphics settings, stable frame rates even on older hardware, buttery-smooth animations, nice effects and shaders and a lot more indicate that the development team has the right people in the right positions, which is no longer a given these days. And even the storytelling and worldbuilding surprise and convince in their own way, even if that might not be that relevant for many DOOM fans. The effort made here to provide an entertaining narrative framework with the help of cutscenes, characters and an admittedly pretty stupid, very flashy, but also comprehensible lore is quite remarkable by DOOM standards. Undoubtedly, I had certain expectations in advance, but DOOM suddenly gets factions, including technologically highly developed aliens who represent, among other things, heaven and hell - I certainly didn't expect that. Similar to the jump puzzles, you can of course turn your nose up at the fact that the DOOM franchise is moving in this direction, but hey, the essence of the game is still to shoot at horrible hell-beasts with completely exaggerated weapons until nothing moves anymore or to press a switch every now and then. That's it. Besides, it's your own fault if you take DOOM more seriously than DOOM takes itself, which makes serious gatekeeping a lot stupider than it already is, when I think about it. Anyway, I really enjoyed all the Easter eggs, plush figures and records (the album artwork...awesome). The only problem I have with the storytelling is that for the majority of the game you're left unsure of what's going on. And although it should be clear that Eternal is based on the events of DOOM 2016 in terms of story, the history of the Slayer as well as the how and why of the events and the factions can only be fully understood by meticulously collecting the lore bits scattered everywhere, which is certainly worthy of criticism, but on the other hand has almost no effect on the gameplay loop. If one wanted to criticize something else, it would be that there is help for every kind of crap. Is it really necessary to be presented with the weak points or strategies for every new enemy or weapon?! Where is the excitement and joy of discovery?! Fortunately, these tutorials, which initially and constantly pop up, can be switched off in the options. I haven't tried the multiplayer mode. 9.5 10

review: На фоне DOOM 2016 это полнейший downgrade, в игре все отвратительно, начиная с вида главного меню до стилистики игры(с интерфейсом у кого-то произошел абстрактный кризис в виде рвоты на палитре), мувмента, переработки демонов, добавления дэшей, которых не хватало в части 16 года, буквально 1 дэша, для улучшения мувмента, хотя с руной на мобильность он там достойнее этого. Но здесь разработчик решил добавить 2 дэша не для улучшения вашей мобильности во время стычек, а потому что карто-дел решил сделать огромные карты и еще большие пропасти, потому что 2 прыжков вам не хватит, как и 2 дэшей добавляются перекладины с ползанием по стенам. Изменение бойовки сделано, для того что бы заставить вас использовать все оружие в игре, а не что вы хотите использовать (отличие от части 16 года). Авто-аим у демонов отдельная история... Переработали получение урона, тут он маленький и хилки что с мобов, что по полу разбросаны хилят крайне много ХП (на фоне предыдущей части), а все для чего это, что бы заставить вас получать в лицо от авто-аима демонов. Колектблс, стал еще хуже чем в первой части, его тут просто тьма, хоть и лазить не нужно до изнеможения как в прошлой части, но этим не лучше. Вообщем и целом, позитивного в этом калле ничего не обнаружил, придется через силу с отвращением до пройти этот бред так, как хотел пройти серию игр. (Разочарование в увиденном словами не передать).

Но здесь разработчик решил добавить 2 дэша не для улучшения вашей мобильности во время стычек, а потому что карто-дел решил сделать огромные карты и еще большие пропасти, потому что 2 прыжков вам не хватит, как и 2 дэшей добавляются перекладины с ползанием по стенам. Изменение бойовки сделано, для того что бы заставить вас использовать все оружие в игре, а не что вы хотите использовать (отличие от части 16 года). Авто-аим у демонов отдельная история... Переработали получение урона, тут он маленький и хилки что с мобов, что по полу разбросаны хилят крайне много ХП (на фоне предыдущей части), а все для чего это, что бы заставить вас получать в лицо от авто-аима демонов. Колектблс, стал еще хуже чем в первой части, его тут просто тьма, хоть и лазить не нужно до изнеможения как в прошлой части, но этим не лучше. Вообщем и целом, позитивного в этом калле ничего не обнаружил, придется через силу с отвращением до пройти этот бред так, как хотел пройти серию игр. (Разочарование в увиденном словами не передать).
review_english: Compared to DOOM 2016, this is a complete downgrade, everything in the game is disgusting, from the appearance of the main menu to the style of the game (someone had an abstract crisis with the interface in the form of vomiting on the palette), movement, reworking of demons, adding dashes that were missing in the part of 16, literally 1 dash, to improve the movement, although with the mobility rune it is more worthy than that. But here the developer decided to add 2 dashes not to improve your mobility during skirmishes, but because the mapmaker decided to make huge maps and even larger abysses, because 2 jumps are not enough for you, just as 2 dashes add crossbars with wall crawling. The change in combat was made in order to force you to use all the weapons in the game, and not what you want to use (difference from the part of 16). Auto-aim for demons is a different story... They reworked the damage received, here it is small and heals that from mobs, that are scattered on the floor, they heal a lot of HP (against the background of the previous part), and all this is for is to make you get punched in the face by the auto-aim of demons. Collectibles has become even worse than in the first part, it’s just darkness here, although you don’t have to climb to the point of exhaustion like in the last part, but it’s no better. In general, I didn’t find anything positive in this calla, I’ll have to forcefully and disgustedly go through this nonsense the way I wanted to go through a series of games. (Words cannot express the disappointment in what I saw).

review:
Очень бодрый шутер. Менеджмент ресурсов - в целом мне понравилась эта механика. Эти "шахматы", когда тебе нужно думать даже в мясном экшене. Думаю, она не понравилась тем, кто заходит исключительно с желанием пострелять-поубивать, без нужды использовать доп. механики для получения ресурсов.

То, что нас "вынуждают" использовать весь свой арсенал - это действительно круто. Обычно в шутерах есть пара крутых стволов, которые используются на постоянке, а про другие даже не вспоминают, тот самый пистолет, который в части 2016 года давался на старте, а после получения дробовика никто про эту тварь не вспоминал. Да, в Eternal тоже по большей части используется только Супердробовик-Баллиста-Ракетомёт (ну, как минимум, я так играю, за других не знаю), но другие пушки тоже идут в ход, та же плазменная винтовка для отстрела щитов солдат, пусть со временем она используется реже, но все же это лучше, чем в других шутанах.

Высокая сложность ой как дает прикурить, прошел на "Кошмаре", умирал много, на "Абсолютный Кошмар" мне пока нервов не хватает, но, может, когда-нибудь попробую. Любимая часть, надеюсь, когда поиграю в The Dark Ages, эмоции будут ничуть не хуже.

review_english: A very cheerful shooter. Resource management - overall I liked this mechanic. These "chess" games when you have to think even in meaty action. I think it was not liked by those who come in solely with the desire to shoot and kill, without the need to use additional ones. mechanics for obtaining resources. The fact that we are “forced” to use our entire arsenal is really cool. Usually in shooters there are a couple of cool guns that are used on a regular basis, but others are not even remembered, the same pistol that was given at the start in the 2016 unit, and after receiving a shotgun no one remembered about this creature. Yes, in Eternal, too, for the most part, only the Super Shotgun-Ballista-Rocket Launcher is used (well, at least that’s how I play, I don’t know about others), but other guns are also used, the same plasma rifle for shooting the shields of soldiers, although over time it is used less often, but it’s still better than in other shooters. The high difficulty gives me a bit of a kick, I beat it on “Nightmare”, I died a lot, I don’t have the nerves for “Absolute Nightmare” yet, but maybe I’ll try it someday. My favorite part, I hope when I play The Dark Ages, the emotions will be no worse.

review:
Лучший Doom в плане геймплея, но в остальном плане тут полно странных решений. Здесь зачем-то добавили головоломки, которые довольно часто портят весь темп. После того как ты перерезал орду демонов тебе хочется побыстрее прирезать ещё одну, а не бегать и пытаться понять куда тебе нужно запрыгнуть. И в итоге оказывается, что тебе вообще надо было под карту куда-то залезть и что-то там нажать. Идея повествования сюжета сквозь записочки это вообще полный бред, гораздо лучше все было реализовано в Doom 2016, где была и резня, и понятный сюжет. Здесь же чтобы понять сюжет нужно с мудрым ебалом сидеть читать 200 страниц кодекса, спасибо. 

Могло быть лучше.

review_english: The best Doom in terms of gameplay, but otherwise it's full of strange decisions. For some reason, puzzles were added here, which quite often spoil the whole pace. After you have killed a horde of demons, you want to quickly kill another one, and not run around and try to figure out where you need to jump. And in the end it turns out that you actually had to crawl under the map somewhere and click something there. The idea of ​​narrating the plot through notes is generally complete nonsense; everything was implemented much better in Doom 2016, where there was both a massacre and a clear plot. Here, in order to understand the plot, you need to sit and read 200 pages of the codex, thank you. It could have been better.

review:
Total AAA slop. Do people actually enjoy this kind of thing? Enjoy being treated like this for money? Playing it feels like you're being talked down to by a bunch of marketing execs. Like the devs don't trust you to understand what a videogame is, and want to hold your hand on a guided tour of their big shiny waste of millions of dollars. The entire thing feels like a shockingly sloppy watered down version of Doom 2016 made by people who had zero idea what made the original appealing, whose only care was juicing up the memetic trailer-worthy crap to try to sell a product.

The guns feel mushy and underpowered, chiefly, I imagine, because glory kills and chainsawing have been "upgraded" from a cool gamification of resource management into a job task mandated by corporate that you need to perform every 3 seconds, which sure sucks the fun out of it. Competent level design and pacing has been eschewed in favour of big, green, always visible GO HERE DUMB ASS waypoints, cutscenes for every time a door opens, and tutorial rooms and pop ups for every new thing you pick up.

The subtle "weight" that everything in 2016 had, from basic combat movement, to things like the feedback from weapons, to even tiny details like acquiring keycards is just gone now. Just blandly gamified into the dirt. The moody and sharply coloured lighting that so often made 2016 gorgeous to look at is now all smoothed over, grayed out, the contrast and atmosphere flattened. The music is barely even noticeable by comparison, feeling more like a generic mixtape constantly droning in the background. The game just has no personality. Just a blockbuster movie droning at you about nothing.

The attempts at a story/dialogue are not only laughably written- the kind of garbage you'd expect out of a multi-million dollar design-by-committee waste- but really feel out of place in a Doom game, especially one that's trying to push itself as some kind of fast paced, adrenaline filled shooter. About the only thing missing to complete the content feeding trough vibe is Doomguy making some MCU-era quips to the demons or spouting gravelly 2006 era mature-game-for-cool-adults one liners. The entire setting has been flanderized to death- where the sporadic glee shown for brutality, blood, and guts in 2016 once stood there's now a feeling of a cheap "appeal to the crowd to move units" philosophy.

It's all cheap, and hollow. The big budget adaptation of a classic book, filtered down into the blandest form imaginable and spoon fed to the audience so they don't accidentally fail to appreciate the money you spent making it. It's a sharp reminder of why I don't often play modern AAA titles, or even feel that interested in them. I'd replay 2016 in a heartbeat, but I doubt I'll ever pick this up again.

review: Doom Eternal, IS ONE HECK OF A GAME. The combat mechanics is deep. A lot of tactics, which enemy to shoot which part of his body, with what weapon, there is no cover, only viceral combat and hectic movement as you avoid some enemies, focus on those you want to kill first and slowly start thining the herd. The battles are fast and furious with pulsating heavy music background, its a trip, pure adrenline rush. While you are gunning down enemies in a fever of range. you also have to manage your inventory of weapons and your health. Replenish ammo by sawing off enemies, setting demons on fire for armour, perform gory kills to get some health back... its a constant combat loop! Run fast, shoot this guy, saww this demon, set this one on fire, strangle this one... on and on the loop goes. SOUNDS hectic, maybe a bit intimidating at first, but once you get the hang of it. the game is pure adreneline and gets your heat pounding. The game does a good job in introducing you to different mechanics, tactics you need so there wasnt really any part I felt frustrated or unfair (I played on ultra violent). The battles aside, you will spend sometime jumping on floating platforms and monkey bars to move to the next location, thats new BUT nostalgic to boomer era games and nicely done, suprisingly that has its own charm. There is story too, but this is Doom who cares, slay them all. If you play shooters and you havent played Doom? You HAVE to!!
